prompt: Chart Amazon.com, Inc.'s (AMZN) annual growth rates for revenue, EBITDA, free cash flow, and EPS over the last 8 fiscal years as a multi-line chart, and call out in the narrative whether AMZN's growth is accelerating or decelerating across each of the 4 metrics year over year.
expectedReason: time-series chart needs enough history to show shape
responsePreview:
The chart above plots Amazon's annual growth rates for revenue, EBITDA, free cash flow (FCF), and EPS over fiscal years 2018–2025. Below is the underlying data and a year‑over‑year acceleration/deceleration breakdown for each metric.

| Fiscal Year | Revenue Growth | EBITDA Growth | FCF Growth | EPS Growth |
|-------------|---------------|---------------|------------|------------|
| 2018        | 30.9%         | 73.7%         | 169.8%     | 221.9%     |
| 2019        | 20.5%         | 33.4%         | 25.2%      | 13.6%      |
| 2020        | 37.6%         | 36.5%         | 19.7%      | 82.1%      |
| 2021        | 21.7%         | 45.9%         | –156.8%    | 54.9%      |
| 2022        | 9.4%          | –48.4%        | –14.7%     | –108.2%    |
| 2023        | 11.8%         | 133.1%        | 290.7%     | 1192.6%    |
| 2024        | 11.0%         | 38.5%         | 2.1%       | 91.9%      |
| 2025        | 12.4%         | 33.5%         | –76.6%     | 28.8%      |

### Acceleration / Deceleration Year‑over‑Year

**Revenue** – Decelerated from 30.9% to 20.5% in 2019, then accelerated to 37.6% in 2020. It decelerated for two consecutive years (2021, 2022) to a low of 9.4%, then accelera...
